fields.definition_origin: Conversational onboarding pattern attributed to Typeform's 'one-question-at-a-time' web form (founded 2012, Barcelona) which inverted the form-first convention. Mobile adaptation traces to Tinder's swipe-card paradigm (2012) repurposed as preference selection, then crystallized in 2017-2020 wellness apps (Noom, Headspace) and reformalized as 'big-button conversational' in Cal AI's 33-step quiz (2023-2024). Form-style descends from HTML <form> + DMV-paper conventions, codified for web by Luke Wroblewski's 'Web Form Design: Filling in the Blanks' (Rosenfeld Media, 2008) and NN/g's form best-practices (Jakob Nielsen, multiple updates 2000-2024). The conversational/form distinction was named in DesignerUp's 'Conversational vs Form Onboarding' teardown (2023) and reinforced in Mobbin/Adapty 2024-2026 reports.
fields.core_mechanism: Form-style asks the user to fill in all required fields on a dense screen — the cognitive frame is 'paperwork.' This activates working-memory load (Sweller 1988) for every visible field simultaneously, triggers form-anxiety (associated with bureaucratic friction — the IRS/DMV mental model), and makes abandonment cheap because zero progress has been committed once the user retreats. Conversational style decomposes the same data collection into one-question-per-screen tap interactions; the user is in dialogue, not paperwork. Each tap is (a) a micro-commitment that compounds sunk cost (Arkes & Blumer 1985), (b) an investment in the IKEA-effect sense (Norton et al. 2012), and (c) a flow-state primer (Csikszentmihalyi 1990) — single-action screens with immediate feedback are flow's textbook ingredients. The mobile-thumb-zone constraint (Hoober 2013) makes form fields awkward on small screens; conversational big-button selection is anatomically optimized.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Form fields activate the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (working-memory orchestration) for every visible field, raising parallel cognitive load. Single-question conversational screens recruit the same circuits but serialize the load, never exceeding the 3-7 working-memory item ceiling (Cowan 2001). Tap-driven binary choices engage the basal-ganglia action-selection loop with low decision-cost, consistent with Hick's Law's logarithmic time scaling (Hick 1952). Confirmation feedback (haptic + animation on tap) provides immediate dopaminergic reward — variable-ratio schedules across question types (item 27) keep the novelty system engaged.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Typeform's internal benchmark (multi-million-form dataset, Typeform State of Forms 2023) reports conversational forms yield 14-43% higher completion rates than equivalent multi-field web forms; effect strongest on mobile. (2) Nielsen Norman Group form research consistently shows progressive single-input designs reduce abandonment vs. dense multi-field forms (NN/g 'Form Design Best Practices', updated 2024). (3) Mobbin teardowns of 2024-2026 top-grossing apps (Cal AI, Flo, Noom, Headspace, Headway, Prayer Lock, BetterMe, Lasta, Fastic, Yazio, MacroFactor) show 100% conversational pattern adoption for top wellness/AI-wrapper onboarding. (4) Adapty 2026 'High-Performing Onboarding': bottom-quartile form-style flows reach paywall at ~25-35%; top-quartile conversational flows reach paywall at 70-85%. (5) Baymard Institute mobile-form research (2022-2024) documents 27% of mobile form abandonment correlates with 'too many fields visible'. (6) Cal AI's 33-step conversational quiz scaled to $30M Y1 ARR (2024) — a form-style equivalent in the same niche (Lifesum's older 8-field form) underperformed by an order of magnitude on trial conversion. No single peer-reviewed RCT compares conversational vs form for mobile onboarding head-to-head; evidence is convergent industry benchmark + foundational psychology.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Step-completion rate per screen, total onboarding completion rate, time-on-step distribution, paywall-reach rate, trial-start conversion, qualitative 'felt like a survey vs felt like a chat' user-research code, keyboard-show rate (proxy for text-input fatigue), thumb-reach failure rate (proxy for off-zone tap targets).
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Typeform internal: +14-43% completion lift conversational vs traditional form. Adapty 2026 implied benchmark: ~2x paywall-reach gap between form-style bottom-quartile and conversational top-quartile. Specific RCT-grade lift not publicly available — most evidence is matched-cohort case study.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Architectural rule: ONE question per screen, ALWAYS. Exception: paired tightly-coupled fields (height-feet + height-inches; first-name + last-name) may co-exist on a single screen, but never more than 2. (2) Big-button selection: tappable cards ≥ 96dp tall, full-width minus 24dp side margin, 16-20px corner radius (varied across system, not uniform — anti-slop), 18-20pt label type. Forbid system Radio/Checkbox primitives — they read as form. (3) Speech-bubble framing optional: small avatar icon left of question header gives 'mascot is asking' chat affordance (Headspace, Duolingo, Cal AI all use). (4) Selection animation: scale + spring + haptic feedback (sensoryFeedback .selection on iOS 17+, HapticFeedbackConstants.CONFIRM on Android). Reanimated 3 spring config {damping: 14, stiffness: 180}. (5) Auto-advance vs manual continue: auto-advance on single-select (Cal AI pattern, 200-300ms delay) for momentum; manual Continue button on multi-select. (6) Keyboard-input minimization: ≤1 free-text input in entire flow; numeric inputs use bespoke wheel-pickers (iOS UIPickerView style) not raw text fields. (7) Forbid system <form>: build typed component tree where each step is a discriminated-union variant (see item 27). (8) Transition between screens: 250-350ms slide + crossfade; never instant cuts (instant cuts read as form-page reload). (9) Persist answers to local store + backend on every transition (item 32). (10) Form-style EXCEPTION justified: mandatory KYC (fintech/identity verification — see item 56), Apple/Google sign-in fallback fields, address entry where structured fields are legally required.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI step 1-33: every screen is single-question with 4 big-button options or compact picker. Step 4 'What's your goal?' shows 4 cards (Lose weight / Build muscle / Maintain / Bulk) at 152x152px, 2x2 grid, illustration-led. (2) Flo period-tracker onboarding: cycle preference, then single-question demographic screens with bespoke wheel-pickers for date-of-birth (no keyboard). (3) Headspace 2024 onboarding: mascot avatar in top-left corner of every question screen, chat-bubble framing, 'Andy' is asking. (4) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: faith-tradition picker → painpoint single-select → identity tile select; conversational throughout, 15-min duration. (5) Duolingo onboarding: language picker, then conversational lesson questions, Duo mascot speech-bubbling each prompt. (6) Headway: genre tile select → goal single-select → identity tile select. (7) Yazio: meal-preference single-select → diet-style tiles → goal slider. (8) Counter-example (form-style): Mint/Intuit budget-app legacy onboarding (pre-2023 flow) presented 6 form fields on one screen — abandonment cratered, redesigned to conversational in 2024. (9) Counter-example (legitimate form-style): Wealthfront KYC step where SSN/address/employment legally required to be co-located.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary; the conversational pattern is engineered for thumb-zone single-tap interaction. Web equivalent: Typeform's one-question-per-page design, but cursor + keyboard-tab navigation makes form-style still viable on web (NN/g notes 'forms are fine on web for utilitarian flows'). Mobile abandons form-style aggressively because keyboard-pop occludes 50%+ of viewport, fields outside thumb zone require hand-shift. Web onboarding for marketing funnels (Headway web checkout) still adopts conversational; web KYC/checkout (Stripe Atlas) still uses form-style multi-field — context-dependent.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Conversational pattern aligned with both 2025-2026 platform languages. Liquid Glass (iOS 26+, WWDC25) bolder left-aligned alert typography fits conversational question headers (28-32pt, weight 350-500). Material 3 Expressive (I/O 25, 18,000-participant study) spring motion + larger shape morphing fits the answer-selected micro-celebration on big-button taps. Both fight against system <form> primitives — bespoke tappable cards align with platform expressiveness. Form-style fights M3E's 4x-faster element-discovery research (dense forms slow scanning) and reads as legacy on iOS 26+.
fields.category_fit: Conversational STRONGEST: wellness/habit, AI-wrapper, fitness, mental-health, fintech-personalization (not KYC), edtech, dating-profile-builder, journaling, gaming-character-creation. Form-style ACCEPTABLE: KYC (fintech identity verification), legal/medical intake forms (HIPAA-bound questionnaires where audit trail matters), B2B admin onboarding (Linear team setup, Notion workspace setup — power users tolerate density), mandatory address-entry checkout. Conversational WEAK fit: utility apps with universal value where any onboarding is over-built (calculator, weather, flashlight); single-feature productivity (just open the editor — Linear, Notion's empty-state seeding).
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. Build 6 typed question-component variants (item 27) in 2-3 days; copy + content authoring + spring-tuning takes the remainder. Server-driven UI (item 31) extends to 2-3 weeks but unlocks zero-deploy iteration.
fields.tooling_options: OnboardJS (open-source typed onboarding state machine), Plotline (server-driven onboarding for mobile, supports conversational flows out of box), Appcues Mobile, Pendo Mobile, Product Fruits, Adapty Onboarding Builder (2026, drag-and-drop conversational flows), Superwall (onboarding paywalls + conversational pre-paywall), Typeform (web). For native build: React Native + XState v5 (item 30) + Reanimated 3 + react-native-mmkv. Avoid: react-native-paper TextInput primitives for onboarding — they read as form.
fields.ai_dependency: none for the pattern itself. Optional: AI-stitching answers across steps (item 14) and AI plan reveal (item 12) layer on top.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude with the architectural constraint explicitly. Prompt fragment 1: 'Build a React Native onboarding flow with the conversational pattern. CONSTRAINT: ONE question per screen. Each screen renders a single typed component from this discriminated union: type Step = { kind: "preference" | "demographic" | "goal" | "painpoint" | "identity" | "social_proof"; ... }. FORBID: a multi-field <Form> component with multiple TextInputs visible simultaneously. FORBID: react-native-paper or system Radio/Checkbox primitives — build custom tappable cards (≥96dp tall, full-width minus 24dp margin, 16px corner radius). FORBID: keyboard-driven text inputs for anything except optional final email/name capture. Use spring selection feedback (Reanimated 3, damping:14, stiffness:180) + sensoryFeedback .selection on iOS / VibrationEffect.EFFECT_TICK on Android. Auto-advance after 250ms on single-select; manual Continue button on multi-select. Use slide+crossfade transitions between steps (350ms).' Prompt fragment 2: 'Audit any onboarding screen against the conversational checklist: (a) is it single-question? (b) is the question header ≥28pt and left-aligned? (c) are answer choices big-button cards, not radios? (d) is haptic feedback wired? (e) does answer-tap auto-advance or require Continue? Output a per-screen audit table; flag any failures and propose fixes.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must be told to design conversational frames, not form pages. Prompt fragment 1: 'Mockup 8 onboarding screens for a wellness app, each ONE QUESTION ONLY. Frame 1 (preference): Question header 28pt Cabinet Grotesk weight 350 left-aligned, 64px top padding, 24px side margin. Below: four tappable cards in 2x2 grid, each card 152x152px, 16px corner radius, 1px border in ink/8, illustration centered with 16pt label below in Söhne Medium. Selected state: 2px ember-orange border #E36B2C, 1.04x scale, soft shadow. NO radio buttons. NO checkbox primitives. Bottom of screen: small step counter "3 of 12" in Söhne Mono 12px. Cream background #F4EFE6. Frame 2 (demographic — birthdate): Question header same style, below a custom three-wheel date picker (month / day / year), each wheel scrolls independently, 88px tall, ink-color text #1A1816. NO keyboard. Frame 3 (painpoint multi-select): Header, six pill-shaped tappable chips, 48px tall, wraps to 2-3 lines, multi-select indicated by filled ember-orange background. Continue button bottom-anchored thumb-zone. Forbid Inter; use Cabinet Grotesk + Söhne. Forbid Lucide icons; use hand-drawn illustration set. Forbid emoji bullets.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Counter-example for compliance reference: ONE form-style screen for KYC where 5 fields (full name / DOB / SSN last 4 / address / phone) co-exist legally — only this screen breaks the conversational rule. Label this screen visually distinct ("Verify your identity" subhead) so the rest of the flow remains conversational.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to a single multi-field <Form> component with React Hook Form + Zod schema validation, rendering all fields on one screen — DMV aesthetic. AI also defaults to system primitives (TextInput, RadioButton, Checkbox) which all read as form. AI auto-imports react-native-paper or shadcn/ui Form components by default. Counter-prompt: 'FORBID react-hook-form. FORBID Zod schema-rendered forms with multiple visible fields. FORBID react-native-paper TextInput, RadioButton, Checkbox. FORBID @hookform/resolvers. Build custom tappable Card components from scratch, one Question per route. Output the per-screen file structure FIRST as a directory tree (one file per question), then implement components. Auto-advance on single-select with 250ms delay using setTimeout cleared on unmount. Use Reanimated 3 spring transitions, NOT React Native default Animated.timing.' AI also defaults to instant cuts between screens — counter: 'Use react-navigation Stack with custom transitionSpec spring + 350ms slide+crossfade — instant cuts read as form-page reload.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Single-screen multi-field form (DMV aesthetic) — 30-50% drop-off in first 3 fields. (2) Conversational pretender — visually conversational but with 3+ free-text inputs that pop the keyboard repeatedly (kills flow on mobile). (3) System primitives leakage — RadioGroup, Checkbox, TextInput all read as paperwork even when wrapped in conversational chrome. (4) Instant-cut transitions — break the chat-rhythm illusion. (5) Forced-auto-advance on multi-select where user can't review their selection before committing. (6) Massive form-style KYC dropped mid-flow without visual differentiation — breaks the conversational expectation set early. (7) Conversational chrome for genuinely-mandatory dense data (legal/medical intake) — wastes screens and frustrates users who just want to fill the form fast.
fields.regulatory_risk: Low-Medium. Conversational doesn't itself raise risk; specific implementations might. (a) Hidden-field collection (auto-collected analytics/IDs alongside conversational questions) must still satisfy GDPR Art. 13 transparency — declare in privacy notice. (b) iOS App Store Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization — every conversational question must serve declared functionality. (c) UK AADC + COPPA 2026: chatty mascot framing on apps for under-18s can be classified as 'engaging but manipulative' if combined with social-pressure copy. Conversational style is generally compliance-positive vs form-style because it improves comprehension (consent screens often poorly read in form) — Article 7 GDPR consent must be 'distinguishable, in clear and plain language' which conversational better serves.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: iOS Apple HIG recommends single-task screens for onboarding (post-iOS 26 alert/modal redesign emphasizes one-decision-per-screen). Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization — conversational makes per-question justification natural. Google Play Data Safety form requires per-question disclosure, easier with conversational. EU Digital Fairness Act draft Q4 2026 favors conversational-with-justification over form-dump because the latter can be classified as 'unbundled aggressive data collection'. No platform forbids form-style outright; both are permitted.
fields.accessibility_implications: Conversational pattern accessibility-positive when done right: each screen contains one focusable interactive region, simplifying VoiceOver/TalkBack flow. Question header should be marked as accessibilityRole='header'. Big-button cards must have explicit accessibilityLabel, not 'image of jogger' but 'Active lifestyle, button'. WCAG 2.2 §2.5.8 target size ≥24x24px (cards typically 96dp+ exceed easily). prefers-reduced-motion: replace spring selection with crossfade. Auto-advance on single-select must NOT bypass screen-reader users — provide explicit Continue button as fallback when accessibility services detected (AccessibilityInfo.isScreenReaderEnabled). Dynamic Type / text scaling: card height must flex with content (use minHeight, not fixed). Custom date pickers (avoiding keyboard) must be keyboard-operable with arrow keys and spell numbers via accessibilityValue.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Conversational fatigue at 30+ steps — Cal AI's 33 is empirical ceiling. (2) Latency on transitions feels broken — pre-render next screen, never blocking spinner between Q1 and Q2 (use optimistic UI). (3) Auto-advance can feel hostile if user changes their mind — 250ms is tight, longer (500ms) for older audiences. (4) Mascot/avatar over-presence creates uncanny-valley effect — Headspace had to dial back Andy's omnipresence in 2018 redesign. (5) Single-question screens multiply codebase complexity — solve with shared template + JSON content (item 31). (6) Keyboard-driven text inputs sneak in via 'edge cases' (free-text Other option) — audit and minimize. (7) On older Android low-end devices, big-button card animations drop frames — disable spring on detected low-end.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Conversational style cross-culturally robust because it mirrors face-to-face dialogue norms. JP/KR: chat-bubble framing aligns with LINE/KakaoTalk-conditioned UI expectations (excellent fit). MENA RTL: question header right-aligned, slide transitions reverse direction, mascot avatar mirrors. CN: Xiaohongshu/Douyin users tolerate higher density — Western single-question minimalism can feel under-used; consider 2-question screens (still tap-driven, no keyboard) for CN market. LATAM: warmer mascot tone outperforms cold UI. Form-style is more culturally portable but always under-performs conversational on mobile globally — Adapty's 62.3% localization-test win rate (item 36) interacts: localized conversational > translated form-style every time.
fields.prescribed_action: Default to conversational: ONE question per screen, big-button tappable cards (≥96dp), spring selection feedback, 250-350ms slide-crossfade transitions between screens. Forbid system <form> primitives, react-hook-form, RadioGroup, Checkbox, multi-field screens. Form-style permitted ONLY for legally-required mandatory dense data (KYC, address-entry checkout, mandatory medical intake) and visually flagged as a distinct phase. Audit every onboarding screen against the conversational checklist before ship.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Foundational — defines the screen-architecture rule that the question taxonomy (item 27) populates. All six question types render under conversational chrome by default; form-style is the explicit exception, not the rule.
fields.aha_relevance: Indirect but load-bearing: conversational pacing accumulates investment one tap at a time, building the sunk-cost ramp into the AI plan reveal. Form-style flattens that ramp because zero per-tap commitment is felt — users would not feel the reveal as 'earned'.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Conversational fits Caregiver (warm chat tone), Magician (transformative dialogue), Lover (intimate one-on-one), Jester (playful banter), Hero (challenge prompts), Innocent (friendly mascot framing). Form-style fits Sage (evidence/precision), Ruler (institutional authority), Everyman (utilitarian) — but only in their narrow appropriate categories (KYC, legal, medical). Mismatched: Outlaw forced into conversational corporate chatty framing reads sanitized; soften with terse sardonic copy register instead of mascot-cheery.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'Wall-of-Text + Cognitive Overload' and 'Single-screen account-creation form' antipatterns. Conversational structurally prevents wall-of-text by per-screen budget. Pairs with item 27 (Question Type Taxonomy) — conversational chrome + 6 typed question variants = the canonical 2024-2026 long-onboarding architecture.
uncertain: specific RCT-grade conversion lift figures comparing conversational vs form for mobile onboarding
